# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Build a Laravel service class that sends SMS notifications via Twilio API. The service should handle authentication and message sending. Configure the class to read Twilio credentials from the appropriate configuration source.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use Twilio\Rest\Client;

class SmsNotificationService
{
    protected $twilioClient;
    protected $from;

    public function __construct()
    {
        $sid = config('services.twilio.sid');
        $token = config('services.twilio.token');
        $this->from = config('services.twilio.from');
        
        $this->twilioClient = new Client($sid, $token);
    }

    public function sendSms($to, $message)
    {
        try {
            $this->twilioClient->messages->create($to, [
                'from' => $this->from,
                'body' => $message
            ]);
            return true;
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            // Handle exception or log error
            return false;
        }
    }
}

// In config/services.php, add the following configuration:
/*
'twilio' => [
    'sid' => env('TWILIO_SID'),
    'token' => env('TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN'),
    'from' => env('TWILIO_FROM'),
],
*/

// Add these Twilio credentials to your .env file:
/*
TWILIO_SID=your_twilio_sid
TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=your_twilio_auth_token
TWILIO_FROM=your_twilio_phone_number
*/
